title: Creamy Croatian Cheese Lasagna
description: A malty, cheese-pull, crowd-pleaser Croatian dish featuring tarragon-french flavors, this lasagna is a jain-friendly option for any occasion. It is a rich and satisfying meal that combines the best of Croatian cuisine with French influences.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the tarragon to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
In a large bowl, combine the french cheese blend, croatian cheese, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the malty beer and a pinch of salt and pepper.
To assemble the lasagna, spread a layer of the beer mixture in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Arrange 4 lasagna noodles on top of the beer mixture.
Spread half of the cheese mixture over the noodles, followed by half of the skillet mixture.
Repeat the layers, starting with the noodles, then the beer mixture, then the cheese mixture, and finally the skillet mixture.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
